title: hypericum barbatum subsp. calabricum
intro: Hypericum barbatum subsp. calabricum is a subspecies of the St. John's Wort plant, a flowering herb native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to have antimicrobial, antiviral, and antifungal effects. The health benefits of Hypericum barbatum subsp. calabricum include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce anxiety and depression, and support wound healing. In herbal preparations, it is often used as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also added to salves and creams for topical application.
